content.problemStatement:
On each of the following NNN days (numbered 111 through NNN), Chef is planning to cook either pizza or broccoli. He wrote down a string AAA with length NNN, where for each valid iii, if the character AiA_iAi​ is '1', then he will cook pizza on the iii-th day, while if AiA_iAi​ is '0', he will cook broccoli on this day.

Chefu, his son, loves pizza but hates broccoli ― just like most kids. He wants to select a substring of AAA with length KKK and change each character '0' in this substring to '1'. Afterwards, let's define pizza time as the maximum number of consecutive days where Chef will cook pizza. Find the maximum pizza time Chefu can achieve.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains a string AAA with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum pizza time.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chefu can choose the substring A[2,3]A[2, 3]A[2,3] = "10", and change the third character of AAA to '1'. Then, the pizza time is 555 days: from day 222 to day 666.
Example case 2: Chefu can choose the substring A[2,4]A[2, 4]A[2,4] = "000". Then, the pizza time is 444 days: from day 111 to day 444.
